key_insight: The paper replaces thresholding on raw nonlinear residuals with online-learned residual structure to identify the difficult components targeted by nonlinear elimination preconditioning.
engineering_relevance: This makes nonlinear solvers for large PDE systems more robust and less sensitive to manual tuning, especially in parallel domain-decomposed settings.
method: Collect residual snapshots during Newton stagnation phases.
Train an unsupervised extractor (PCA or autoencoder) to reconstruct the dominant residual pattern.
Use the reconstructed residual to define the bad subset and apply nonlinear elimination locally on each subdomain.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports lid-driven cavity flow tests up to Reynolds number 10,000, where the learned approach produced more coherent bad subsets and improved convergence over the baseline NE preconditioner.
watch_item: The online learning step adds model-training and reconstruction overhead, and performance may depend on snapshot quality and subdomain-wise model stability.
paper_title: A domain decomposition online-learning-enhanced nonlinear elimination preconditioner
